data.title: CZ: CEXs Have Zero Reason for Involvement with Terrorists
data.description: Binance founder and former CEO, Changpeng Zhao (CZ) posted on X in response to a federal judge's recent dismissal of a civil lawsuit that sought to hold Binance and founder and CZ liable for transactions that allegedly helped terrorist groups conduct attacks. CZ stated in his post that terrorist groups would be unlikely to generate revenue for exchanges because they typically do not engage in active trading, which is where exchanges earn fees. At most, they might deposit funds and immediately withdraw them, and these are transactions that produce little or no income for the platform.

data.title: Dormant Whale Stakes 8,208 ETH with Kiln Finance
data.description: A dormant cryptocurrency whale has reactivated after a year, staking 8,208 ETH with Kiln Finance. According to BlockBeats, the whale had accumulated these ETH over the past four years at a cost of $16.09 million, currently realizing a profit of $768,000.

data.title: PRECIOUS METALS | China's Gold Reserves Increase for 16th Consecutive Month
data.description: China's gold reserves have continued to grow, reaching 74.22 million ounces by the end of February, according to the People's Bank of China. This represents an increase of 30,000 ounces from the end of January. According to NS3.AI, this marks the 16th consecutive month of rising gold holdings, bringing the total reserves to approximately 2,308.5 tons.

data.title: XRP Spot ETFs Experience Significant Outflow in U.S.
data.description: On March 6, U.S. XRP spot ETFs saw a substantial net outflow totaling $16.6191 million. According to NS3.AI, this data indicates that investors withdrew this amount from XRP ETF products within the United States during that trading day.

data.title: Robinhood's publicly traded fund fell 16% on its first day of trading on the NYSE, having raised less than its target.
data.description: Robinhood's newly launched Robinhood Ventures Fund I debuted on the NYSE this week, but closed at $21, a 16% drop. The fund, which aims to open up investment opportunities in popular private equity firms to retail investors, had a $1 billion fundraising target but has only raised $658.4 million so far (up to $705.7 million if underwriters exercise their full subscription rights). The fund's holdings reportedly include companies such as Databricks, Stripe, Mercor, Ora, Ramp, Airwallex, and Revolut, but its lack of exposure to widely anticipated high-valuation soon-to-be-listed companies (such as OpenAI, Anthropic, and SpaceX) is considered a major reason for the lack of retail interest. Robinhood stated that it plans to expand the fund to 15-20 high-quality late-stage growth companies in the future and is actively seeking to add equity to the portfolios of popular startups such as OpenAI. (TechCrunch)

data.title: Survey Reveals U.S. Adults View National Morality Negatively
data.description: A recent survey conducted by Pew Research Center highlights a unique perspective among U.S. adults regarding national morality and ethics. The Long View, institutional investor, posted on X. The survey, which included participants from various countries, found that the United States stands out as the only nation where a majority of adults perceive the morality and ethics of their fellow citizens negatively. This finding underscores a significant divergence in how Americans view their societal values compared to other countries. The results suggest that political factors may be influencing these perceptions, contributing to a more critical view of national ethics within the U.S. compared to global counterparts.

data.title: Anthropic's Claude Opus 4.6 Model Identifies Significant Firefox Vulnerabilities
data.description: 23pds posted on X. Anthropic has announced that its Claude Opus 4.6 model has discovered 22 vulnerabilities in Firefox within a span of two weeks. Of these, 14 are classified as high-risk, accounting for nearly one-fifth of the high-risk vulnerabilities expected to be addressed throughout 2025. Mozilla has assigned 22 CVE numbers to these vulnerabilities.

data.title: Coinbase Prime launches unified margin feature, covering spot, derivatives, and regulated perpetual contracts.
data.description: Coinbase announced the launch of regulated futures and unified cross-product margining for its Coinbase Prime service, covering the crypto spot and derivatives markets. This will provide 24/7 access to over 20 futures contracts through Coinbase Financial Markets, while integrating with Coinbase's existing perpetual contracts. This feature allows institutions to share margin within a single account, improving the capital efficiency of hedging strategies, simplifying portfolio management, and supporting unified risk assessment for spot and futures positions. This will enable trading, custody, financing, and risk management to operate in a unified environment.

data.title: Akash Network Proposes Burn-Mint Equilibrium Model for AKT
data.description: Akash Network has announced the commencement of an on-chain vote today, March 7, regarding a proposal to introduce a Burn-Mint Equilibrium token-burning model. According to NS3.AI, if the proposal is approved, a network upgrade to implement the model is planned for 2:00 p.m. UTC on March 23. The proposed design aims to enhance the token's direct utility by burning all AKT used for deployments, thereby linking its value more closely to network usage.

data.title: The People's Bank of China has increased its gold reserves for the 16th consecutive month.
data.description: The People's Bank of China reported that China's gold reserves stood at 74.22 million ounces (approximately 2,308.5 tons) at the end of February, an increase of 30,000 ounces (approximately 0.93 tons) month-on-month. At the end of January, reserves were 74.19 million ounces (approximately 2,307.567 tons), marking the 16th consecutive month of increases. (Jinshi)

data.title: The US SOL spot ETF saw a total net outflow of $8.2255 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (March 6th, Eastern Time), the total net outflow of SOL spot ETFs was $8.2255 million. Among them, the Invesco Galaxy Solana ETF (QSOL) saw a net inflow of $426,900, bringing its historical total net inflow to $2.1473 million. The Fidelity Solana Fund ETF (FSOL) experienced a net outflow of $4.9974 million, bringing its historical total net outflow to $10.2586 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of SOL spot ETFs was $807 million, with an equity ratio of 1.66%, and a historical cumulative net inflow of $958 million.

data.title: The US XRP spot ETF saw a net outflow of $16.6191 million in a single day.
data.description: According to SoSoValue data, yesterday (March 6th, Eastern Time), XRP spot ETFs saw a total net outflow of $16.6191 million. The XRP spot ETF with the largest net outflow yesterday was the 21Shares XRP ETF (TOXR), with a net outflow of $10.6014 million, bringing its total historical net outflow to $10.6014 million. The second largest was the Bitwise XRP ETF (XRP), with a net outflow of $3.6484 million, bringing its total historical net inflow to $7.1215 million. As of press time, the total net asset value of XRP spot ETFs was $983 million, with an XRP net asset ratio of 1.18%, and a cumulative historical net inflow of $1.236 billion.

data.title: Japan Seeks Exemption from U.S. Tariff Increase on Automobiles
data.description: Japan has requested the United States to exempt it from a proposed tariff hike on automobiles, which is set to rise from 10% to 15%. Bloomberg posted on X, highlighting concerns that the increased duties could adversely affect Japan's automobile industry. The request comes amid ongoing trade tensions and fears of economic repercussions for Japan's automotive sector, a significant contributor to the country's economy. The potential tariff increase is part of broader trade measures being considered by the U.S. administration, led by U.S. President Donald Trump, aimed at addressing trade imbalances. Japan's appeal underscores the global impact of U.S. trade policies and the importance of international negotiations in mitigating economic disruptions.

data.title: CFTC and SEC Consider Relocation to Shared Washington Complex
data.description:
The Commodity Futures Trading Commission (CFTC) and the Securities and Exchange Commission (SEC) are reportedly in discussions about relocating to the same building complex in Washington, D.C., according to BlockBeats. This complex is situated near Union Station and a short distance from the U.S. Capitol.

Sources familiar with the matter indicate that the plan does not involve merging the two regulatory bodies, which oversee different markets. Instead, the discussions focus on moving the CFTC into the building currently occupied by the SEC. The relocation talks began last year, and implementation might not occur until 2027 at the earliest.

The U.S. General Services Administration, responsible for overseeing federal real estate, is also involved in these discussions.

data.title: Pakistan Just Passed a Crypto Law Covering 40 Million Users and $20 Billion in Digital Assets
data.description:
Pakistan’s National Assembly passed the Virtual Assets Act 2026 on March 5, ending years of legal ambiguity for one of the world’s largest informal crypto markets. The legislation creates a dedicated regulatory authority, mandates licensing for all crypto service providers, and introduces a capital gains tax on digital asset conversions.
source: https://www.ethnews.com/pakistan-just-passed-a-crypto-law-covering-40-million-users-and-20-billion-in-digital-assets/

data.title: Software Stocks Achieve Historic Outperformance Against Semiconductor Sector
data.description:
The Kobeissi Letter posted on X. Software stocks have recently experienced a significant surge, with the Tech-Software ETF, $IGV, outperforming the Semiconductor Sector ETF, $SOXX, by 16.59 percentage points over the past six trading sessions. This marks the largest six-day outperformance on record. During this period, $IGV has rallied by 8.37%, reaching its highest level since February 2nd, while $SOXX has declined by 8.22%, nearing its lowest point since February 5th.

This development represents a notable reversal from the end of January when software stocks underperformed by nearly 15 percentage points, the largest gap since 2008. The recent surge in software stocks coincides with an increase in hedge fund short exposure to U.S. software and services stocks, which reached a record 3.8% last week. As a result, a short squeeze in software stocks has occurred, contributing to their historic outperformance against the semiconductor sector.

data.title: U.S. President Trump to Honor Fallen Soldiers at Dover Air Force Base
data.description: U.S. President Donald Trump announced plans to visit Dover Air Force Base with the First Lady and cabinet members to pay respects to fallen soldiers. According to Jin10, this visit underscores the administration's commitment to honoring military service and sacrifice. The President's itinerary includes a solemn ceremony to acknowledge the contributions of the nation's military personnel.

data.title: Top Global Asset Management Firms' Bitcoin Holdings Revealed
data.description:
Bitcoin treasury company Strategy has disclosed the top ten holdings of global asset management firms on the X platform. According to PANews, the list is led by The Vanguard Group, which holds an 8.12% stake valued at $3.183 billion. Capital Research & Management follows with a 7.7% stake worth $3.019 billion. BlackRock Fund Advisors holds a 3.64% stake valued at $1.428 billion, while another holding by Capital Research & Management is at 2.62%, valued at $1.026 billion.

SSGA Funds Management has a 2.29% stake worth $897 million, and Morgan Stanley holds a 2.08% stake valued at $815 million. UBS Securities owns a 2.02% stake worth $793 million. Amundi Asset Management SASU holds a 1.77% stake valued at $693 million. Geode Capital Management has a 1.46% stake worth $573 million, and Norges Bank Investment Management rounds out the list with a 1.32% stake valued at $517 million.

data.title: Whale Address Opens Leveraged ETH Short Position
data.description: On March 7, a whale address identified as '0x218A' deposited 2.18 million USDC into HyperLiquid, according to BlockBeats. The address then opened a 10x leveraged short position on ETH. Previously, this whale had profited over $11.8 million through trading activities.

data.title: US Private Credit Market Faces Decline Amid AI Concerns
data.description:
The Kobeissi Letter posted on X. The U.S. private credit market is experiencing a downturn, with the median listed Business Development Company (BDC) now trading at just 0.73 times its net asset value (NAV), marking the lowest point since 2020. BDCs, which are publicly traded entities lending to small, mid-sized, and distressed U.S. businesses, provide retail investors access to private credit markets. Currently, they are valued at 73% of their claimed worth.

Over the past 18 months, the median listed BDC price-to-NAV ratio has been steadily declining. For context, this ratio plummeted to approximately 0.35 times during the 2008 financial crisis, indicating that the market valued BDCs at merely 35 cents on the dollar.

This decline occurs amid growing concerns that artificial intelligence (AI) could disrupt the business models of software firms, complicating efforts to refinance debt. The average BDC holds around 20% exposure to the software sector, which is under scrutiny due to potential AI impacts.

The private credit industry is facing significant pressure as these challenges unfold, raising questions about the future stability and valuation of BDCs in the market.
